title: Java Class Encapsulation
description: Practice the pre-record immutable class idiom with private fields, a constructor, and an explicit getter.
learningGoals: Type a constructor for an immutable class.
Differentiate explicit getters from Java record accessors.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if another class runs account.owner = "Bo";?
choices: It updates the owner to Bo
It updates owner only if getOwner was called first
Compilation fails because owner is private
completionChecks.explanation: owner is private, so it is not accessible outside Account; the assignment fails to compile, and final would also forbid reassignment inside the class.

completionChecks.prompt: How can Account.owner be changed after construction?
choices: It cannot be reassigned by this class because the field is final
Any caller can assign it because getOwner exists
It changes whenever getOwner is called
Only a subclass can change it
completionChecks.explanation: final means owner is assigned once during construction and cannot be reassigned; private also hides it from callers, so getOwner only reads it.

completionChecks.prompt: What does new Account("Ada").getOwner() return in the same package?
choices: Compilation fails because the constructor is not public
completionChecks.explanation: The constructor assigns the parameter into the private final field with this.owner, and the getter returns that stored value.
